howItWorks.intro: This emergency food storage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Cal = (Adults x Cal/Day + Children x Cal x 0.6) x Activity x Days
Food Weight ~1,500 calories per pound (average dried food)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What foods are best for long-term storage?
faq.answer: Top staples for long-term storage: white rice (30+ years), dried beans (25+ years), wheat berries (30+ years), rolled oats (25+ years), powdered milk (20+ years), honey (indefinite), salt (indefinite), and freeze-dried meals (25+ years). All require cool, dry, dark storage.

faq.question: How many calories should I store per day?
faq.answer: Store at least 2,000 calories per adult per day. Active adults need 2,500-3,000. Children need 1,200-1,800 depending on age. In cold weather, increase by 25-50% as your body burns more calories to stay warm. Always store more than minimum.
